title: Inheritance FAIL: child removes Gate 5 from kernel gate policy (GATE_POLICY_INCOMPATIBLE)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates GATE_POLICY_INCOMPATIBLE: the child cert family declares gate_policy=[0,1,2,3,4] — removing Gate 5 (canonical hash / Merkle integrity). Gate 5 closes the replay chain and makes certs tamper-evident. A family extension that strips Gate 5 breaks the audibility guarantee inherited from the kernel: any cert produced by this family can no longer be verified as unmodified by an external auditor. The inheritance edge is invalid because a family_extension must include at least all the gates its parent requires. This is the canonical gate-policy obstruction certificate.

validation_checks.description: parent family schema version is a recognised QA cert family
child family schema version is a recognised QA cert family
validation_checks.details: 'QA_AREA_QUANTIZATION_CERT.v1' is a registered family_extension (this fixture models a bad version of [108] that stripped Gate 5)

validation_checks.description: child gate policy is superset of parent gate policy
validation_checks.details: child=[0,1,2,3,4] is missing Gate 5 from parent=[0,1,2,3,4,5]; missing={5}

validation_checks.description: child failure algebra extends parent without deleting required types
validation_checks.details: parent types all present; child adds DOMAIN_SPECIFIC_FAIL

validation_checks.description: child logging contract includes move, fail_type, invariant_diff
child preserves_invariants refs resolve against parent invariant names

fail_ledger.message: extracted_child.gate_policy=[0,1,2,3,4] is not a superset of extracted_parent.gate_policy=[0,1,2,3,4,5]. Missing gate: 5 (canonical hash / Merkle integrity). Gate 5 closes the replay chain and makes certs tamper-evident. A family_extension must include all gates required by its parent kernel. Removing Gate 5 means any cert produced by this hypothetical family cannot be independently audited for tampering — the inheritance edge is structurally invalid.
